calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-09-2010 Continued To Mar-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court noted that Mr. Sullivan was not present. Mr. Ferdon stated that he spoke with Mr. Sullivan's associate, that they were not objecting to payment of the taxes, that the IRS and the Franchise Tax Board would not talk to the accountant, and asked if he could get an order allowing payment of the Franchise Tax Board's creditor's claim so that he can get a release/certificate. The Court directed Mr. Ferdon to file an Ex Parte request for payment of the Creditor's claim with agreement from Mr. Sullivan. The matter was continued to March 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 status of filing of the Petition for Final Distribution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Continued To Feb-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Continued To Jan-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-09-2009 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Directing Public Administrator To File Petition For Final Distribution Or Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the Administrator. John Alkazin for the Petitioner.

Mr. Ferdon stated that there were problems with the Internal Revenue Service and the Franchise Tax Board, that he would have a withholding and that he would go ahead with the Petition for Final Distribution. Status on filing of said petition was set for December 9, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the petition For Order Directing The Public Administrator To File The Petition For Final Distribution, (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Jung.

Petition For Order Directing Public Administrator To File Petition For Final Distribution Or Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-09-2009 Continued To Oct-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. John Alkazin for the Petitioner.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the tax liability was at $13,246.00, that they were waiting for a response from the Internal Revenue Service regarding penalties and that the Franchise Tax Board also filed a Request for Special Notice. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that there was a pending issue regarding the Heirs as opposing Counsel did not provide a death certificate or a birth certificate when submitting the Heirship Affidavit. The matter was continued to October 14, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the tax liabilities and for status on the information received regarding the Heirs.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (875 Vermont Street Unit 6, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Sep-10-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Adminstrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that the site listed as Unit 6 on the petition was also known as Unit 201 and that the parking space used by the Decedent was G instead of I. The sale was confirmed at $395,000.00. No bond is required. The Court granted the petition, (subject to a written order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
